issue_title: Strings that contain an integer starting with 0 are converted into integers
issue_body:
For instance, if you have a parameter in your template with a value that is a string "012345" it is silently converted into the integer 012345 after aws cloudformation package action. 

This can raise issues:
- if the parameter is expecting a string as a value.
- if you use condition function to compare this value with other ones.
- if you use such a string as a key in a Mapping section (map keys have to be strings).

Type tags, such as !!str, are not preserved by the package action, so they cannot be used as a workaround.

issue_title: Set throttling and all of the other parameters supported by StageDescription
issue_body:
Hi @sanathkr,

Would it be possible to specify all of the parameters for an Api deployment, e.g. per-stage (or even per-method) ThrottlingRateLimit etc.? `AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment` already supports that, but it doesn't update Stage when re-deploying the Api. So if we benefit from automatic redeployments by `AWS::Serverless::Api`, we have to sacrifice control over throttling etc. Would it be possible to enable full functionality for the latter?

EDIT: also, I don't really want to enable UsagePlans just so I could control throttling. That would require specifying an API key in addition to normal authentication parameters, which would increase burden on my clients.

Thanks!

issue_title: set returned by `discover()` can mutate
issue_body:
Recently, my program failed with the following error:

``` none
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./sonos-button.py", line 85, in <module>
    main()
  File "./sonos-button.py", line 52, in main
    coordinators = find_coordinators(zones)
  File "./sonos-button.py", line 79, in find_coordinators
    for zone in zones:
RuntimeError: Set changed size during iteration
```

This appears to have happened because `soco.discovery.discover()` does not return a static set, but rather [returns a reference to a set managed by a zone object](https://github.com/SoCo/SoCo/blob/22e787dad105ddb2814dfd4cd1d4d76e2349dc4a/soco/discovery.py#L177). This means that when the zone changes, the set's contents can change as well. And if the set's contents change in a separate thread due to an event, the above error can happen.

My suggestion would be to either change the documentation to make it clear that the returned set is dynamic and should be copied before iteration, or return a copy of the zone's set. (I have a slight preference for the documentation change since having a dynamic set is handy.)

issue_title: Data Item I048/020, Target Report Descriptor: XPP missing
issue_body:
At least in version 1.21 of the CAT048 specification the Data Item I048/020, Target Report Descriptor contains the XPP bit in the first extend. So

```xml
<Bits from="7" to="6">
	<BitsShortName>spare</BitsShortName>
	<BitsName>spare bits set to 0</BitsName>
	<BitsConst>0</BitsConst>                       
</Bits>
```

should be

```xml
<Bits bit="7">
	<BitsShortName>spare</BitsShortName>
	<BitsName>spare bit set to 0</BitsName>
	<BitsConst>0</BitsConst>                       
</Bits>
<Bits bit="6">
	<BitsShortName>XPP</BitsShortName>
	<BitsName>XPP</BitsName>
	<BitsValue val="0">No X-Pulse present</BitsValue>
	<BitsValue val="1">X-Pulse present</BitsValue>
</Bits>
```

issue_title: Speeding up array serialization
issue_body:
This is related to the issue opened here ( https://github.com/materialsproject/fireworks/issues/97 ). It seemed relevant to raise this here if `monty` plays a role in how `fireworks` performs serialization. There are few relevant points to raise here.

1. Current serialization is too slow, something like `tostring` would be much faster (but less readable).
2. Serialization could be more compact using some form of fast compression. (`blosc` would be worth considering)
3. Large arrays will not fit into a single document in MongoDB. (could use GridFS or an external file system with filenames saved in BSON)

The first point could be implemented very easily, but `tolist` remains the more readable solution. So, it would be worth making using `tostring` optional somehow. As the name suggests, this returns a string, which is acceptable in JSON

The second point is less important, but may help avoid having to deal with the third point immediately. Using `blosc` is relatively straightforward, there are already nice Python bindings for this ( https://github.com/ContinuumIO/python-blosc ). The benchmarking included already shows how simple this is to do. It is worth noting that `blosc` is very fast and is designed for frequent transmission. This returns a string, which is acceptable in JSON.

The third point may take more or less work depending on the strategy chosen. An easy way to solve this would be to use HDF5 outside of MongoDB and simply store the filenames with dataset in the MongoDB documents. HDF5 has its own ways to do compression, but third party compression strategies can be used, as well. Here `h5py` is probably the best way to go if HDF5 is selected. The alternative could be using GridFS (compression would be tricky). Being able to adjust block sizes would be nice if GridFS is selected.

issue_title: Sending multi-image DFU over serial fails due to incorrect packet sequence
issue_body:
I've been testing serial DFU with an customized version from branch 0.5.1 which supports recording the packet output stream. It appears there may be a defect related to packet sequence numbering for the case where the DFU ZIP file used for the DFU update contains more than one image.

For example, if nrfutil is first used to build a DFU ZIP file containing bootloader, softdevice, and application, then the resulting file contains two images: 1) a BL+SD image, and 2) an APP image.

If this same DFU ZIP file is provided to nrfutil to initiate a serial DFU, the serial transport does not reset the packet sequence number between sending the two images. This results in a somewhat "random" staring sequence number for the second image (depending on how many packets are in the first image modulus 8). In my case, I was producing two HCI packet recording files which I then replay to a serial bootloader, but the second one never was accepted correctly since it didn't begin with sequence 1. I corrected the problem by setting the HCiPacket.sequence_number = 0 within the serial transport open() function. (Maybe close would have been a better location, I'm not sure.) In any case, that produced two recording files where both of them have sequence 1 for the first packet and that seems to work.

issue_title: Autobahn in thread
issue_body:
While trying to create a publishing only loop on a separate thread to push progress reports on a process, I ran into trouble trying to run the loop on a separate thread. 

`ValueError: set_wakeup_fd only works in main thread`

The problem comes from the ApplicationRunner trying to add a signal handler on the loop in the thread, which in turn triggers a test verifying that the signal is not handled anywhere but in the main thread.

```python
try:
    # set_wakeup_fd() raises ValueError if this is not the
    # main thread.  By calling it early we ensure that an
    # event loop running in another thread cannot add a signal
    # handler.
    signal.set_wakeup_fd(self._csock.fileno())
except (ValueError, OSError) as exc:
    raise RuntimeError(str(exc))
```

It would be nice to be able to be able to:

1.  toggle the handling of the SIGTERM by the runner using an argument,

issue_title: Rename Model.decode to something else
issue_body: `Model.decode` was originally intended to separate some kind of decoding from the training done in `Model.forward`.  However, we realized later that we really needed that decoding to be done inside `Model.forward`, so you could get proper validation metrics, so `Model.decode` now basically only converts integers to strings and otherwise makes the output human-readable.  We should deprecate `Model.decode` and replace it with a better-named method that makes it clear it's only for translating the output to something consumable by a human.

issue_title: six version is pinned to 1.9.0
issue_body: Because the `requirements.txt` sets `six==1.9.0`, projects that have multiple dependencies on six that use only a lower bound may fail to run because a newer version 1.10.0 might be installed as a result of processing some other dependency and pip is apparently not clever enough to figure all this out on its own.  Any specific reason why the requirement couldn't be `six>=1.9.0` ?

issue_title: Controller/Builder not creating new releases.
issue_body:
We've got a 1.12 cluster that has been running for a while.

Yesterday, one of our applications was sitting on v52.  We attempted to release a new version of the application as normal.  We pushed to git, the builder ran and reported success at the end, only it reported we had just deployed v52.  Looking in the `deis releases` output for this application showed no record of the deploy we'd just made, just v52 from a few weeks ago when we last released.

We've since tried a few of our other applications - one of them did not show this behaviour, however most of them have since started showing this behaviour (including one application that was successfully deployed yesterday).

I can find no evidence of problems with the controller - it's logs show that it's getting called to by the builder as expected at the end of the builds, and I can't find any other evidence of problems.

The builder is a bit trickier to diagnose - every service on the machine appears to be running fine, except I'm no longer able to ssh in to it to check the logs from the builder.

Any ideas what could be causing this?

issue_title: deis-controller generated fleet units should [X-Fleet] Conflicts with the others of the same app
issue_body:
When there is an instance failure, units migrate to the remaining fleet machines. Over time, as fleet machines fail and are terminated by the AWS auto-scaling group and replaced with new instances,  this tends to leave the longest-running fleet machine with a good chance of having multiple if not all units for an app on it.

This just happened to me this evening. I had a 10 minute production outage where the deis-registry, deis-store-gateway, and one of my critical web apps had all 3 units somehow allocated on the the same fleet machine which suddenly stopped responding (thanks AWS!) The 3 app web units were trying to be relocated by fleet, but they were trying to docker pull from the deis-registry which was waiting on the deis-store-gateway to finish starting up which was waiting on ceph which was running in that normal degraded slow way ceph does when one of its monitors/OSDs isn't up. It did eventually repair itself, but that web app wasn't being serviced in the interim.

While it might be fair to say "well fleet shouldn't schedule all 3 on the same machine!" - how is Fleet to know?

At the moment, deis-controller generates fleet units without any kind of affinity for the Fleet scheduler to know better than to allocate all of the units of an app on the same fleet machine.

While using `deis tags` would afford us the ability to tag generated units with fleet machine affinity, it still doesn't prevent the occurrence of a fleet tagged machine from acquiring multiple or all units for an app. Meaning: fleet will merrily schedule multiple units to any available fleet machine with the app's tags.

What we need is a way of tagging deis-controller generated fleet units with an [X-Fleet] Conflicts.

For example, a myapp v3 web proctype unit would ideally have this appended to it:

    [X-Fleet]
    Conflicts=myapp_v3.web.*

Granted, on an N instance deis fleet cluster, this limits it to `deis ps:scale web=N`. Any more than N, and the units simply won't get scheduled by fleet.

As Conflicts is a simple glob, in order to allow overcommits of units, we would need to introduce a "oversubscription" ordinal component to the generated unit service name.

For example, if myapp were on a 3 machine fleet cluster, and we set `deis ps:scale web=6`, in order to allow for oversubscription of 2 (and only 2) units on any given fleet machine, we would need to generate service names like:

    myapp_v3.web.1.1
    myapp_v3.web.1.2
    myapp_v3.web.1.3
    myapp_v3.web.2.1
    myapp_v3.web.2.2
    myapp_v3.web.2.3

And have a unit [X-Fleet] Conflicts something like:

    [X-Fleet]
    Conflicts=myapp_v3.web.1.*
    Conflicts=myapp_v3.web.2.*

If the second ordinal is the modulus of N (given an N machine fleet cluster), the first ordinal would be a dividend, and would reflect this oversubcription.

Depending on the memory size limit of each unit, this will likely require some deis client support of setting the maximum oversubscription of an app unit per machine as well. Something like:

    deis ps:maxoversubscription web=2

This would imply that, on a fleet cluster of N machines,  if I were to set scale greater than N*2, that the additional units beyond the maxoversubscription dividend would end up spreading across the cluster as per usual fleet rules (as there would be no applicable X-Fleet Conflicts glob to prevent it).

Anyway, this is all conjecture, I'm sure OpDemand will contrive something that solves these kinds of problems.

Thanks!

issue_title: Fix list-commands output to handle long command names
issue_body:
I assumed for the recent rewrite that it would be okay to assume command names were under 16 chars. That's pretty good, but we have a violator:

```
=== globus transfer endpoint ===

    search          Search for Globus Endpoints
    show            Display a detailed Endpoint definition
    deactivate      Deactivate an Endpoint
    create          Create a new Endpoint
    update          Update attributes of an Endpoint
    my-shared-endpoint-listList all Shared Endpoints on an Endpoint by...
    server-list     List all servers belonging to an Endpoint
    autoactivate    Activate an Endpoint via autoactivation
    delete          Delete a given Endpoint
```

`my-shared-endpoint-list` is a bit of a long name, and probably should be fixed, but that doesn't mean it should break `list-commands` so easily.

Probable solution is to do a line break and indent the short-help, like so:
```
=== globus transfer endpoint ===

    search          Search for Globus Endpoints
    show            Display a detailed Endpoint definition
    deactivate      Deactivate an Endpoint
    create          Create a new Endpoint
    update          Update attributes of an Endpoint
    my-shared-endpoint-list
                    List all Shared Endpoints on an Endpoint by...
    server-list     List all servers belonging to an Endpoint
    autoactivate    Activate an Endpoint via autoactivation
    delete          Delete a given Endpoint
```

We can do that for any command that's going to come too close to the right-hand column (within 2 chars?).

issue_title: Context objects should be revamped
issue_body:
Context objects (like `Device`, `Operation`, etc) should be revamped somewhat to read/write from a shared dictionary rather than being merged in from the TelemetryChannel.  There are two reasons why we want this:

* `TelemetryContext` objects need to be easily copied.  This is important to properly support operations, where we'll want a copy of the `TelemetryClient` per operation, accessible via thread-local storage (which then allows actual dependency tracking).
* `dict.update` is far more performant than the current copy loop that occurs for *each* telemetry item.

issue_title: Check the Kademlia pong results for hash of the ping
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The Kademlia p2p pong is supposed to be the hash of the ping, according to page 4 of [this (PDF) paper], which has a nice summary of the p2p protocol. The paper claims that without this check, "*pong* messages [could be] sent from spoofed IP addresses".

Here's where I expected a check:

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/eb7138ae67caab09c16d886f59e54639ad97b522/p2p/kademlia.py#L425-L433

Is this implemented somewhere else, or some reason that we don't need the check?

### How can it be fixed

Check the pong result against the hash of the ping, failing the pong if it is mismatched.

Successfully installed certifi-2018.1.18 chardet-3.0.4 cytoolz-0.9.0 eth-hash-0.1.0 eth-utils-1.0.1 idna-2.6 requests-2.18.4 toolz-0.9.0 tqdm-4.19.6 urllib3-1.22
``` 

## How can it be fixed?

Only check for the back end and runtime rather than import time. For big libraries like eth-utils, they may never want to run `keccak`, so warning about the missing backend is a nuisance with little upside.

We can do a lazy load of the backend, and throw up at the first `keccak` call, if necessary.

issue_title: "--no-channel-priority" has no effect on build number
issue_body:
The `--no-channel-priority` cmd line flag (and the `channel_priority: false` setting) only affect the version numbers, but not the build numbers, which is IMHO unexpected and should be fixed.

Suppose you have two channels *A* and *B* with the following packages:

## Channels

**A**
- spam-1.0-py36_0
- eggs-1.0-py36_0

**B**
- spam-1.1-py36_0
- eggs-1.0-p36_1

## Installation with channel priority

`conda install spam eggs --channel=A --channel=B` will install both packages from *A*

## Installation without channel priority

`conda install spam eggs --channel=A --channel=B --no-channel-priority` will install *spam* from *B* because it is newer, but *eggs* from *A* although it has a higher build number and is thus newer than the version in *A*

## `conda info`

issue_title: conda install --yes flag ignored when --use-local set with conda-build 2.1.6
issue_body:
@Lnaden commented on [Fri Mar 10 2017](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1825)

Trying to execute `conda install --yes --use-local {package}` from a package built with `conda-build` ignores the `--yes` flag and waits for user input. This [has](https://travis-ci.org/choderalab/openmmtools/jobs/209789665#L951) put a [halt](https://travis-ci.org/choderalab/pymbar/jobs/209453492#L646) to trying to do automatic builds as they time out, I am only using the 2 links as examples.

I can replicate the problem on my local machine and on downgrading to `conda-build` 2.1.5 the problem goes away, even without re-building the package. 

---

@msarahan commented on [Fri Mar 10 2017](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1825#issuecomment-285718752)

Sorry, but it looks like a coincidence that conda-build 2.1.5 works but 2.1.6 breaks.  Your build is succeeding (https://travis-ci.org/choderalab/openmmtools/jobs/209789665#L919), but it is only the conda step after that that is broken.  I'm going to move this issue to the conda issue tracker.

Also, this is concerning: https://travis-ci.org/choderalab/openmmtools/jobs/209789665#L925

you should probably add a pin to whichever jpeg you actually want, so that conda does not arbitrarily choose something you don't want.

issue_title: conda 4.3* breaks package cache & dependency resolution
issue_body:
Since conda 4.3* has been released we have (once again) been forced to pin conda to an older version (4.2*). Various problems arise:

* Dependency resolution has changed; older packages are preferred by conda 4.3*; couldn't trace this problem to its cause as the dependency resolution codebase is hard to understand and therefore to debug
* Package cache is broken; `CondaError: No package '***' found in cache directories.` is an error that pops up across the board on all our projects that are under CI for various different packages.

Whereas I normally could reproduce problems quite easily, I find that these errors are almost random.

We also frequently see `WARNING conda.gateways.disk:exp_backoff_fn(47): Uncaught backoff with errno 41` popping up on systems that aren't doing anything else, with completely clean & fresh conda installs.

IMHO the changes in conda 4.3* are a violation of semver as they appear to have involved many big changes (e.g., removal of locks) that affect the entire conda codebase. Behaviour has changed very significantly, and thus should have been tested more rigorously before being released. Have you considered working with specific groups of users to beta-test conda's bleeding edge in the field?

Question/Doc enhancement: Bringing VUnit runner functionality to existing testbenches
issue_body:
How to use VUnit for completely new testbenches is pretty well documented, starting with the most basic cases shown in the user guide.

I think a common questions for someone new to VUnit is: _What do I need to do to adapt existing/legacy testbenches to utilize VUnit?_

Developing an automated testbench is good practice, so it is safe to assume that users out there already have test code that was written without VUnit in mind. Without VUnit, a common practice would probably be to lump a variety of tests into a single testbench for a single entity. The testbench is then run and failures are reported via assertions, and the testbench is automatically stopped if the severity is high enough. If there are no failures, the testbench would end automatically (after a fixed duration, or with a call to `std.env.finish`).

Assuming someone has a set of existing testbenches that are written in such a manner, what is the path of least resistance to adopting the VUnit python running framework (automatic compilation, test detection, parallel simulation, reporting results via XML)?  Obviously some of the benefits of the VUnit VHDL test library will be missed, but for legacy code this is acceptable -- the testbench is already complete.

issue_title: Alias remove action tries to remove an index from alias even if it's not part of the alias (and then fails)
issue_body:
Using an action to remove older indices from an alias, works fine as long as there is at least one index removed. If none of the indices to be removed is part of the alias anymore, curator fails.

Example:

config:
```
~# cat test2.yml 
actions:
  alias-today-remove:
    action: alias
    description: "Remove indexes from today alias"
    options:
      name: today
      ignore_empty_list: True
    remove:
      filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: logstash-
      - filtertype: age 
        direction: older
        source: name
        timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
        unit_count: 1
        unit: days

issue_title: Weekly indicies
issue_body:
I've just started using weekly indices (Kibana speak [myindex-]GGGG.WW) with my ELK stack, but finding each component has a different idea of week numbers:

Kibana: http://momentjs.com/docs/#displaying/format/
Logstash: http://joda-time.sourceforge.net/apidocs/org/joda/time/format/DateTimeFormat.html
Curator: https://docs.python.org/2/library/datetime.html#strftime-and-strptime-behavior

Would it be possible for curator to support the ISO8601 style week/year system, which is well defined and also what Kibana uses?

issue_title: Problem validating JSON document against schema
issue_body:
The apm server keeps dropping certain python agent requests with the following error:
```
2018-01-17T13:41:51.252+0100	ERROR	[request]	beater/handlers.go:384	error handling request	
{"request_id": "104c6f2b-8e7a-4480-aca7-cc292b890b4e", "error": "Problem validating JSON document against schema: I[#] S[#] doesn't validate with \"transaction#\"\n  I[#] S[#/required] missing properties: \"service\""}
```
Since it's a schema requirement error, and the schema definition [here](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/master/transaction-api.html#payload-with-transactions) specifies that the service field should be present , i thought this was the best place to post the issue

issue_title: [DNS] Change 'az network dns record update-soa' to 'az network dns record soa update'
issue_body:
Under 'az network dns record', we have a bunch of subgroups (one for each record type) followed by a one-off command 'update-soa'.

The SOA record set is a bit of a special case, in that it can be edited but doesn't support add/remove.

However, I think there's a cleaner way to support this. Remove the 'update-soa' command, and move it to an 'update' command under the 'soa' command group

That way, under 'records' we have a clean list of command groups, one for each record type including SOA. Under each record type, we support add/remove commands, except for SOA which supports an update command instead.

issue_body:
The pertinent code is [here](https://github.com/Azure/msrestazure-for-python/blob/master/msrestazure/azure_active_directory.py#L405-L413), per the latest suggestion from AAD group, for federated accounts we should use ws-trust to acquire tokens from ADFS on-prem instead of directly posting the request to the AAD endpoint, which will not work in new tenant.

3. Deprecate this

issue_title: KeyError: <aiohttp.connector._TransportPlaceholder>
issue_body:
I'm not sure if this is a bug in aiohttp, but if it's a bug in my code, this error doesn't tell my anything:

```
  File "/home/ich/code/stig/stig/client/aiotransmission/rpc.py", line 328, in __post
    response = await self.__session.post(repr(self.__url), data=data, headers=self.__headers)
  File "/home/ich/code/aiohttp/aiohttp/helpers.py", line 102, in __await__
    ret = yield from self._coro
  File "/home/ich/code/aiohttp/aiohttp/client.py", line 233, in _request
    conn = yield from self._connector.connect(req)
  File "/home/ich/code/aiohttp/aiohttp/connector.py", line 386, in connect
    self._acquired.remove(placeholder)
KeyError: <aiohttp.connector._TransportPlaceholder object at 0x7f270bc78c88>
```

So, `placeholder` is added to `self._acquired` (line 379) and then removed again (line 386), but inbetween it has already been removed, hence the `KeyError`.

The culprit is `self._create_connection(req)` (line 382), and eventually `self._loop.create_connection()` (line 732), and then I'm losing track.

Anyway, a simple `if placeholder in self._acquired:` inserted in line 386 seems to fix the issue for me. Or it could be something wrong with my code, but then I still have no idea what it is I'm doing wrong. I'd appreciate any pointers what could cause this.

I can reproduce, but unfortunately not with simple, sharable code.

issue_title: Fix left cell border
issue_body:
The blue/green 5px left cell border, which we have been styling with gradients is having trouble in master. We saw the same issues with JuptyerLab (we copied the styling). The symptoms are:

* As you resize the browser window, the width of the left border oscillates between 1-6ish px.
* The left border in multiselected mode is up at 7px

We have found a fix in JupyterLab that uses the `:before` pseudoselector to provide a nice clean way of implementing this. Here is the PR on JuptyerLab:

https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyterlab/pull/1690

We should port this over to notebook before 5.0 is released.

@gnestor

issue_title: TimeStampType critically broken
issue_body:
I've just reviewed `TimeStampType`, and it seems to be performing quite interesting date arithmetic:

```python
def date_to_timestamp(cls, value):
    ...
    return (delta.days * 24 * 3600) + delta.seconds + delta.microseconds
```

So yeah, it's adding microseconds on top of seconds. :koala: This means that, if the datetime includes microseconds, the results are essentially random timestamps 0 to 999999 seconds (~12 days) into the future from the intended time.

Also, while `DateTimeType` creates timezone-naive datetimes, if such an object is passed to `TimeStampType.to_primitive()`, it assumes local time. This is not a very good assumption because the results then depend on where the application happens to be deployed. I say either require the `tzinfo` component or assume UTC when it's missing. Any sane application should store datetimes in UTC anyway.

My first instinct to fix this is by removing the `types.temporal` module altogether and putting a working `TimeStampType` directly in `types.base`. This way, developers using the class will hopefully be alerted to discover documentation about these issues.

I presume the separate `temporal` module exists because of the dependency on `dateutil`, but that's not needed for anything besides getting the local timezone, which we shouldn't even be doing.

issue_body:
Hi, 

I was getting nans at zeroafter evaluating the gradient of a function that was using np.linalg.norm(x)**2. Couldn't figure it out. After an embarrassingly long time doing other checks, I tried replacing it with np.sum(x**2) which seemed to work fine. Just wanted to bring it to your attention. 

Thanks, 
Arjumand

Sample Code: 
`import autograd.numpy as np 
import autograd as au 

def f_sum(x):
    return np.exp(-1.0*np.sum((x**2)))

def f_norm(x):
    return np.exp(-1.0*np.linalg.norm(x)**2)

df_sum = au.grad(f_sum)
df_norm = au.grad(f_norm)

x = np.zeros((2,1))

print "f:", f_sum(x), f_norm(x)
print "using sum:"
print df_sum(x) 
print "using norm:"
print df_norm(x)
`

issue_title: Refactoring Chunking in the Caller
issue_body:
I want to be able to use the toil-vg wrapping of vg call in my HGVM pipeline, but the current way chunking is done doesn't work for me. I have a single big graph with a few connected components, with multiple primary paths that VCF variants can be on, and I want to run that through vg call. But right now, the call wrapping targets all need a single path name, a single path offset, and a single path length.

Since in the future we probably want vg graphs to be able to hold multiple disconnected segments, with offsets, of a single underlying path, I think we ought to redesign some of the jobs that work on chunks to think like that. Each chunk can have zero or more path segments in it (though maybe only one of any given path, for now), and those path names will be fed to vg call as references, and the variants that come out on those paths will have offsets applied and will be clipped to the right parts of the paths and then can be merged.

To make this work, we need:

* [ ] Support in `vg call` for multiple offset, length, and contig name override parameters.
* [ ] Support in toil-vg for not overriding any contig names, lengths, or offsets (and just using what the caller detects).
* [ ] Support in toil-vg for feeding in multiple paths, with their own optional offsets and lengths, and getting back calls on all those embedded paths from `vg call`.

Then I will be able to feed in a whole graph with no particular paths or offsets defined, and get back the more-or-less-raw calls, and the existing toil-vg call pipeline will be able to feed in a boring per-chromosome linear run of chunks and get back the calls it expects in that context.

Other features that I want here are:

* [ ] The ability to pass a pileup to the caller directly, instead of a GAM.
* [ ] The ability to pull an augmented graph out from the `vg call`-wrapping job.
* [ ] The ability to collect Loci instead of VCF files (although `vg call` emits its Loci in the augmented graph and thus chunking before augmenting would create a big ID collision problem).

issue_title: Support `@seed` decorator
issue_body:
## Problem

Sometimes I ran tests with some random values that pass.
When the seed changes during the next test run I might run into some errors.

Consider this imaginary example:

```python
import random

def will_fail(x):
    return x / random.randint(0, 10)

def test_will_fail():
    assert isinstance(will_fail(5), float)
```

The test will pass for some seeds, but later I will run into the situation when this test will fail.
So, I will fix the code, like this:

```python
def will_fail(x):
    return x / random.randint(1, 10)
```

And I want to be sure that this won't happen again.

## Current solution

I can manually run `random.seed` and set seed values from the previous failing tests.

## Proposed solution

I see this as a huge API feature, that will cover these cases.
It may look something like this:

```python
@pytest.mark.seed('your-seed')  # we had failures with this seed in the past!
@pytest.mark.seed('your-other-seed')  # this seed is also breaking our code
def test_will_fail():
    ... 
```

By doing this we can change the global random seed for a single test. We can do it multiple times. And then return back to normal tests execution.

This API is inspired by `hypothesis`: https://hypothesis.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reproducing.html?highlight=seed

issue_title: Dashboard should not use Google Analytics by default
issue_body:
Currently all activity in the dashboard is sent to Google Analytics. I don't think this is appropriate for the following reasons:

- Many sites probably don't want to track dashboard activity at all anyway.

- Dashboard activity is completely different from front-end (end-user) activity and mixing them up results in skewed metrics for both (the bounce rate on the dashboard is almost 0 for example, but the conversion rate is also 0...).

- Even if you did want to track activity on the dashboard you should do so under a different analytics property so as not to conflate this with front-end activity.

On that basis I think we should override the `tracking` block in the dashboard templates by default.

issue_title: Jupyter >= 4.4.0 is required
issue_body:
Running `python -m matlab_kernel install` assumes that Jupyter provides a
`kernelspec` command, which has apparently been
[introduced in `jupyter_client==4.4.0`](https://github.com/jupyter/jupyter_client/commit/6c361d84d631c4af04b3b8b82d222dc7d704149e)

If `jupyter-kernelspec` is not available, installation of the MATLAB kernel fails with::

    ubuntu@master001:~$ jupyter --version
    4.2.1

    ubuntu@master001:~$ sudo python -m matlab_kernel install
    /opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/IPython/nbformat.py:13: ShimWarning: The `IPython.nbformat` package has been deprecated. You should import from nbformat instead.
      "You should import from nbformat instead.", ShimWarning)
    jupyter: 'kernelspec' is not a Jupyter command

I guess this should be listed in the dependencies or at least mentioned in the README.

issue_title: TypeError raised when casting InputPeerSelf to any kind of int in utils.get_peer_id
issue_body:
I am using the latest asyncio branch 06af73e  ：

```
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_client.py", line 2385, in get_entity
    for x in inputs
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_client.py", line 2385, in <listcomp>
    for x in inputs
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/utils.py", line 462, in get_peer_id
    _raise_cast_fail(peer, 'int')
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/utils.py", line 82, in _raise_cast_fail
    type(entity).__name__, target))
TypeError: Cannot cast InputPeerSelf to any kind of int.
```
[This line of doc](https://github.com/LonamiWebs/Telethon/blob/06af73ed653062444d60205fb97151e7ed032e53/telethon/telegram_client.py#L2456) says `get_input_entity` will return InputPeerUser, InputPeerChat, or InputPeerChannel, but [the actual code ](https://github.com/LonamiWebs/Telethon/blob/06af73ed653062444d60205fb97151e7ed032e53/telethon/telegram_client.py#L2459) return InputPeerSelf, so [this line](https://github.com/LonamiWebs/Telethon/blob/06af73ed653062444d60205fb97151e7ed032e53/telethon/telegram_client.py#L2384) will raise TypeError when calling `utils.get_peer_id` with `InputPeerSelf`.

Should it be changed to `return await self.get_me(input_peer=True)`?

issue_title: inputBinding.valueFrom for File[] is appending items instead of replacing the input value
issue_body:
For reference I'm using OSX, Python 2.7.12, and cwltool v1.0.20170224141733

I'm trying to wrap a `zip` command with multiple files as input.  The input to the job is just an array of source files.  The goal is to have the source files at the root of the resulting zip with no sub-directories.

By default when the command is rendered it passes the input files with some sort of temporary path like `/private/var/folders/9h/j5_q_5hx4511j4blrvs9vc6c0000gq/T/tmpSJzLPX/stg767105dc-13b4-403d-8663-4c6c6133d663/hello.txt`  This results in a zip file that contains a long nested folder.

My solution is to use `InitialWorkDirRequirement` to stage the input files in the output folder [as documented here](http://www.commonwl.org/v1.0/UserGuide.html#Staging_input_files_in_the_output_directory).  I can get this to work if my input is a single file to zip but if I want to use an array of files then I'm having difficulty.

To stage multiple input files I'm using `valueFrom` in the `inputBinding` with a java script expression that iterates the inputs and returns an array with just the base file name.

**params.yaml**
```YAML
zipFileName: myzip.zip
srcFiles:
   - class: File
     path: hello.txt
   - class: File
     path: bye.txt
```

**zip-cwl**
```YAML
cwlVersion: v1.0
class: CommandLineTool
baseCommand: zip

requirements:

  - class: InlineJavascriptRequirement
  - class: InitialWorkDirRequirement
    listing: $(inputs.srcFiles)

inputs:

  zipFileName:
    type: string
    inputBinding:
      position: 1

  srcFiles:
    type: File[]
    inputBinding:
      separate: false
      position: 2
      valueFrom: |
        ${
          var r = [];
          for(var i=0; i < self.length; i++) {
            r.push(self[i].basename);
          }
          return r;
        }

outputs:
  zipFile:
    type: File
    outputBinding:
      glob: $(inputs.zipFileName)

```
**Directory Stucture**
```
$ ls -ls
total 32
8 -rw-r--r--  1 gmetzker  staff   18 Mar  9 16:11 bye.txt
8 -rw-r--r--  1 gmetzker  staff   17 Mar  9 16:09 hello.txt
8 -rw-r--r--  1 gmetzker  staff  107 Mar  9 19:08 params.yaml
8 -rw-r--r--  1 gmetzker  staff  615 Mar  9 19:08 zip-files.cwl
```

The problem seems to be when the zip command is rendered it is supplying 4 files. Two with the long temporary paths and two with the base paths.  It seems like the array return in the javascript expression in `valueFrom` is being appended to the original input value.

```
$ cwltool zip-files.cwl params.yaml
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/bin/cwltool 1.0.20170224141733
Resolved 'zip-files.cwl' to 'file:///Users/gmetzker/cwl-playground/zip-files/zip-files.cwl'
[job zip-files.cwl] /private/var/folders/9h/j5_q_5hx4511j4blrvs9vc6c0000gq/T/tmpBTqPdM$ zip \
    myzip.zip \
    hello.txt \
    bye.txt \
    /private/var/folders/9h/j5_q_5hx4511j4blrvs9vc6c0000gq/T/tmpBWzng6/stg0deafc0c-c165-45ae-ae10-054a20b5a91a/hello.txt \
    /private/var/folders/9h/j5_q_5hx4511j4blrvs9vc6c0000gq/T/tmpBWzng6/stg88d3ead1-a6de-4c61-b5a2-168bed4b910f/bye.txt
  adding: hello.txt (stored 0%)
  adding: bye.txt (stored 0%)
  adding: private/var/folders/9h/j5_q_5hx4511j4blrvs9vc6c0000gq/T/tmpBWzng6/stg0deafc0c-c165-45ae-ae10-054a20b5a91a/hello.txt (stored 0%)
  adding: private/var/folders/9h/j5_q_5hx4511j4blrvs9vc6c0000gq/T/tmpBWzng6/stg88d3ead1-a6de-4c61-b5a2-168bed4b910f/bye.txt (stored 0%)
[job zip-files.cwl] completed success
{

    "zipFile": {
        "checksum": "sha1$4a137c63007d39c830d2a4491cdf7f864d4c6f36",
        "basename": "myzip.zip",
        "location": "file:///Users/gmetzker/cwl-playground/zip-files/myzip.zip",
        "path": "/Users/gmetzker/cwl-playground/zip-files/myzip.zip",
        "class": "File",
        "size": 1092
    }
}
Final process status is success
```

And here is the resulting zip file.  You can see the two extra files with the long paths.
```
$ unzip -vl myzip.zip
Archive:  myzip.zip

 Length   Method    Size  Ratio   Date   Time   CRC-32    Name
--------  ------  ------- -----   ----   ----   ------    ----
      17  Stored       17   0%  03-09-17 16:09  354e5501  hello.txt

issue_title: [marshmallow plugin] Inspect validators
issue_body:
Many of marshmallow's validators can be inspected to provide additional data when constructing OpenAPI entities.

``` python
from marshmallow import fields, validate
from apispec.ext.marshmallow.swagger import field2property

field = fields.Int(validate=validate.Range(min=3, max=42))

prop = field2property(field)
assert prop['minimum'] == 3
assert prop['maximum'] == 42
```

issue_title: Windows does not enumerate COM ports associated with other devices
issue_body:
I have a USB cellular modem which has 2 COM ports associated with it, one shows up in the device manager under the Ports (COM & LPT) the other under Modems. Performing serial.tools.list_ports only shows the one in the Ports. 

The port under the modems is actually the more important one as it is the one needed to send AT commands to control the modem. I can connect and send/receive data using arduino's serial monitor which properly enumerates the port.

issue_title: TCRecommender uses string keys to refer to int item IDs
issue_body: The TCRecommender implementation always returns a dictionary with string keys, even if the model used int item IDs. So for models trained with int item IDs, the resulting Core ML model will throw (unwrapping optional nil) when accessed from Swift, since the cast to `[Int64: Double]` will fail.

issue_title: Use Alias Route 53 records instead of CNAMEs
issue_body:
http://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/DeveloperGuide/resource-record-sets-choosing-alias-non-alias.html?tag=duckduckgo-d-20

Why change from CNAME to Alias records?
* Alias records allow you to use Senza with your “root” team domain (e.g. you can create a stack serving “myteam.example.org”)
* Alias records allow having TXT or MX records with the same domain name
* Alias records avoid one DNS lookup (clients need to lookup CNAME, afterwards resolve the target to the IP)
* You can optionally enable “Evaluate Target Health” to have a DNS failover (AWS will only direct traffic to the Alias target if ELB health is OK)

issue_title: Incorrect model
issue_body:
Hi, I'm currently working on a testing tool that makes use of Z3 to generate models that respect several constraints.
I found that when trying to generate models that contain arrays (of Int to any sort) Z3 fails to provide a model, returning as status UNKNOWN and giving as the reason "incomplete quantifiers" however, it seems to generate a model almost correct, with just a slight error.

For instance, imagine the scenario in which I want to generate a model that contains an array of integers in increasing order of length at least 4 and whose elements are greater than 0, smaller than 40 and multiple of 8. When looking on these constraints we see there is just one possible solution (i.e. [8, 16, 24, 32]).

The code I have is the following:

```
(set-option :produce-proofs true)

(declare-datatypes () ((ArrayWithLength.Int (ArrayWithLength.Int_IT (items (Array Int Int)) (length Int)))))
(declare-datatypes () ((Test (Test_IT (array_integer ArrayWithLength.Int)))))
(declare-fun R.request.body () Test)
(assert (let ((a!1 (forall ((i Int))
             (let ((a!1 (and (< 0 i)
                             (< i (length (array_integer R.request.body)))))
                   (a!2 (< (select (items (array_integer R.request.body))
                                   (- i 1))
                           (select (items (array_integer R.request.body)) i))))
               (and (=> a!1 a!2) (>= (length (array_integer R.request.body)) 0)))))
      (a!2 (forall ((__i_1__ Int))
             (let ((a!1 (and (<= 0 __i_1__)
                             (< __i_1__ (length (array_integer R.request.body)))))
                   (a!2 (> (select (items (array_integer R.request.body))
                                   __i_1__)
                           0))
                   (a!3 (< (select (items (array_integer R.request.body))
                                   __i_1__)
                           40))
                   (a!4 (mod (select (items (array_integer R.request.body))
                                     __i_1__)
                             8)))
               (=> a!1 (and a!2 a!3 (= a!4 0)))))))
  (and (>= (length (array_integer R.request.body)) 4) a!1 a!2)))

(check-sat)
(get-info :reason-unknown)
(get-model)
```

(set-option :produce-proofs true) is there so when running in command line Z3 returns a model with (get-model).

ArrayWithLength.Int is a datatype I created so I can control the length of the array, I know Array is an infinite structure but in this way when reading the generated model I know the array is of a specific length (I'm considering indexing starts at 0).


When running that in command line Z3 returns:

```
 unknown
 (:reason-unknown (incomplete quantifiers))
 (model 
   (define-fun R.request.body () Test
     (Test_IT (ArrayWithLength.Int_IT (_ as-array k!4999) 4)))
   (define-fun k!4999!5001 ((x!0 Int)) Int
     (ite (= x!0 2) 24
     (ite (= x!0 1) 16
     (ite (= x!0 0) 8
     (ite (= x!0 3) 32
       24)))))
   (define-fun k!5000 ((x!0 Int)) Int
     (ite (>= x!0 1) (ite (>= x!0 3) 3 1) 0))
   (define-fun k!4999 ((x!0 Int)) Int
     (k!4999!5001 (k!5000 x!0)))
```

When looking in the generated model k!4999!5001 seems absolutely correct (the important indexes for me 0 to 3 map to the expected integers, other indexes return to 24, that is no problem for me, I just ignore all those indexes outside 0 to 3).
The problem is k!5000 which is used to map the index I provide when I want to obtain the value at a specific index into a different index, that is no problem, but (ite (>= x!0 3) 3 1) is the problem, it is saying that all indexes greater than or equal to 3 will just point to 3 (absolutely ok) but the else says that all indexes between [1, 3[ will point into 1 (e.g. the function will make 2 map to 1) which in this scenario would return me the array [8, 16, 16, 32] which is incorrect.

Please note that the first code I generated using the Java API of Z3.

Note: I'm using the commit cb2d8d21071b9e895158adc4b0a3e8dd052e41a4 of master branch.

May you provide some feedback about this? Am I doing something wrong or is it a bug of Z3?

issue_title: Is it possible to get rid of implict float conversions in django-money?
issue_body:
Hi,

the current implementation appears to implicitly convert certain data types to float [at](https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/b4b4e09e9f33dab6615cd0dc92161fbd2f6de004/djmoney/models/fields.py#L65) [some](https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/b4b4e09e9f33dab6615cd0dc92161fbd2f6de004/djmoney/models/fields.py#L257) [point](https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/b4b4e09e9f33dab6615cd0dc92161fbd2f6de004/djmoney/templatetags/djmoney.py#L81). 

Considering the [instability](http://0.30000000000000004.com/) of a computer's FPU, it would be nice to get rid of these occurrences and use Decimal instead where practicable in order to circumvent inaccurate calculations with monetary numbers because that would be *really* bad.

issue_title: An in-range update of redux is breaking the build 🚨
issue_body:
## Version **3.7.0** of [redux](https://github.com/reactjs/redux) just got published.
Branch Build failing 🚨
Dependency redux
Current Version 3.6.0
Type dependency
This version is **covered** by your **current version range** and after updating it in your project **the build failed**. redux is a direct dependency of this project **this is very likely breaking your project right now**. If other packages depend on you it’s very likely also breaking them. I recommend you give this issue a very high priority. I’m sure you can resolve this :muscle:
Status Details - ❌ **continuous-integration/appveyor/branch** Waiting for AppVeyor build to complete [Details](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/nteract/nteract/build/1.0.2930) - ❌ **continuous-integration/travis-ci/push** The Travis CI build could not complete due to an error [Details](https://travis-ci.org/nteract/nteract/builds/243894832?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification)
---
Release Notes v3.7.0

Another long break!

Oh, hey! I didn't see you sitting there. You look bored. How about a Redux release to spice things up?

Not a huge set of changes to report here. The biggest change, and the reason for the minor bump, is the UMD build is now done via Rollup. One big advantage is more readable code in the bundle. Rollup does "scope hoisting", which is a fancy term for putting every module at the top level of the file. Other than a surrounding IIFE, all of the code in Redux all lives together. You can compare the two here:

Rollup UMD build
vs
Webpack UMD build

There is also a cost savings of 30,811 vs 26,880 bytes, and 6,999 vs 5,995 bytes minified. Redux is already a small library, and this helps shave some extra bytes for our UMD users.

One thing to note is that Webpack has introduced it's own scope hoisting feature in 3.0 beta. So, this isn't intended as an indictment of Webpack. You should continue to use it in your own apps. The adage of "Webpack is for apps, Rollup is for libraries" definitely holds true. It still has a superior developer experience with hot module reloading and webpack-dev-server. But use whatever makes sense for your project, not just whatever we use. 😄

We're also looking at applying this to the NPM bundle. The main motivation is again more readable code in your bundles. Instead of transpilation oddities from Babel, you will end up with a single clean file, which should be easier to read through and debug. It's currently scheduled for the big, mythical 4.0 release and you can follow along in #2358

Changes

  • Build UMD with rollup (#2283 by @TrySound)
  • Warn when bindActionCreators encounters non-function property (#2279 by @artgillespie)
  • Update variables to ES6 (#2169 by @dfrownfelter)

issue_title: scroll behaviour when you execute a cell
issue_body:
From what I understand, it seems like the notebook will auto-scroll to the next input cell on `shift-enter`.  

There are a couple common scenarios where I find this behaviour pretty frustrating:
- When the output takes more than 200ms to render. Jupyter will scroll to the next input cell, and then the output will render and the view will shift further downwards. This pushes the viewport in between cells which is pretty disorienting. 
- When I actually want to view the output of my cell. I press shift-enter and then I have to actively scroll upwards to see my output.
- When I'm happy with my viewport and I'm re-running the same cell over and over with different inputs. Running a cell isn't "idempotent" from a scroll point of view - I can't run a cell twice and preserve my viewport.

Here is an example:

![scroll-behaviour](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1280389/18651185/c040ff42-7e97-11e6-9a13-e8edc7311f21.gif)

What if running a cell didn't adjust the scroll position?

issue_title: Selenium2Library architecture changes
issue_body:
Hi

We are doing some proof on concept work for the Selenium2Library new architecture. The architecture is based on idea to use Robot Framework dynamic library API and remove Selenium2Library multi inheritance from the library. Also we are planning to introduce a context object which would contain information from the current driver/browser. Also we are thinking to introduce a Base class, which could contain example the logging methods and some other common functionality for the library. The Base class would be inherit by other keyword classes, but the idea of the Base class is still little bit in the air and I am not yet sure is it good idea to implement. 

The new architecture is based on this idea: https://github.com/robotframework/PythonLibCore and we did get the PoC working for `Open Browser` and Close All Browsers` keywords. More details about the PoC code can be example found from here: https://github.com/aaltat/robotframework-selenium2library/blob/new_arc/src/Selenium2Library/__init__.py#L179,  https://github.com/aaltat/robotframework-selenium2library/blob/new_arc/src/Selenium2Library/keywords/browsermanagement.py#L76 and https://github.com/aaltat/robotframework-selenium2library/blob/new_arc/src/Selenium2Library/keywords/browsermanagement.py#L37 

By doing the new architecture we might introduce changes which breaks the AngularJSLibrary support for the Selenium2Library release 2.0. I agree that your idea to extend the Selenium2Library with AngularJSLibrary to support AngularJS is a good idea. Therefore is there something that we could do on the Selenium2Library side, which would make easier for AngularJSLibrary to support Selenium2Library 2.0 release.

issue_title: Support for redshift UNLOAD to S3
issue_body:
Parking this here, as it doesn't look like it's supported. 

Redshift will unload potentially multiple CSVs to an s3 [prefix](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/r_UNLOAD.html). This is supported by the [redshift dialect](http://sqlalchemy-redshift.readthedocs.io/en/latest/commands.html#sqlalchemy_redshift.commands.UnloadFromSelect) just like the CopyCommand that's used.

Happy to look into this, if you have any pointers.

I also asked [here](https://github.com/blaze/odo/issues/34#issuecomment-215593436) to connect the conversations.

issue_title: Missing HRV-channel StackedAreaDefinition for native_msg-reader
issue_body:
#### Code Sample, a minimal, complete, and verifiable piece of code

```python
f = MSG4-SEVI-MSG15-0100-NA-20180502124243.556000000Z-NA.nat
nat = Scene(f, reader='native_msg')
nat.load(['HRV'])
tmp = nat.resample("seviri_0deg")
tmp.show(param)
```
#### Problem description
Resampling native format SEVIRI HRV-channel onto `seviri_0deg`-area produces a strange looking result. HRV channel requires a special `StackedAreaDefinition` as it is scanned in two parts.

#### Expected Output
Correctly resampled HRV channel ;)

issue_title: What's the usage of image_blender.py?
issue_body:
Hi, 

What's the correct usage of image_blender.py? 
Usage: image_blender.py -b BASE -t TEXTURE -o OUTPUT [-w WAVELET] [-l LEVEL] [-m MODE]
b and t are just the name of the images; but what about -w, -l and -m? Are these numbers? e.g. -m 1, or are they letters? e.g. -m L, etc?
Leaving -m says it requires an argument
TypeError: Image mode must be 'L', 'P', 'RGB' or 'CMYK' 
putting any of these does not solve the problem.

issue_title: Unable to import credstash module within python lambda function
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm trying to import credstash into a python lambda function like so:
`import credstash`

and access a secret:
`credstash.getSecret('bla')`

And I get the following error upon invocation of the lambda:
`Unable to import module 'ec_lambda_adx_scanner': /var/task/cryptography/hazmat/bindings/_constant_time.so: invalid ELF header`

ec_lambda_adx_scanner is my lambda function name. I'm not running in a virtual env, just deploying and invoking as usual from AWS.

Thank you in advance.


EDIT:
To give you a little more information, I am first installing credstash from a requirements.txt file via a shell script. This builds credstash locally on my Mac. I then use serverless to package up the lambdas and their dependencies and deploy. I believe the issue is that I have built a version of credstash for my Mac environment, but when I try to use that same build on lambda (Linux image) it fails.

Update: I rebuilt the credstash lib on a Linux EC2 AMI instance rather than on my mac and have now been receiving this error instead (seems to be another C lib.):
`Unable to import module 'ec_lambda_adx_scanner': libffi-a7faab9c.so.6.0.4: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory`

issue_title: Sessions in Manage Endpoints and Manage sessions not in sync
issue_body: The sessions in manage endpoints tab are not in synch with the sessions in manage sessions tab in widget UI as show below. The session 6 has been deleted from manage endpoints as shown below, but the UI continues to display the session in the endpoint list. Even the manage sessions UI displays the deleted session as below: This is primarily due to the methods that are used in spark controller. ### Method called when deleting session from manage endpoint widget _Class: Spark Controller_ `delete_session_by_id(endpoint, session_id) -calls-> creates a new LivySession object, say ls with LivyReliableHTTPClient and does ls.delete()` This method doesn't go through the session manager route and hence the sessions in session manager continue to exist even after being deleted. The status shown in manage sessions tab ### Method called when deleting from manage sessions widget _Class: Spark Controller_ `delete_session_by_name(name) -calls-> session_manager.delete_client(name)` This method cleanly deletes the session by removing session from the sessions managed by session manager. So, to make the experience consistent the method **delete_session_by_id** needs to be updated to go through session manager route.

issue_title: IR fps decrease
Endianness of Depth Raw Data from "Save snapshot" Button
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D415 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.09.14.00 | 
| Operating System & Version |  Win 10  |  
| Platform                                 | PC |
| SDK Version                            |  2.13.0                       |  

### Issue Description
Hi, I have an issue with the endianness of depth raw data obtained from the "Save snapshot" button of RealSense Viewer. Reading it as little endian gives the correct depth values for each pixel but doesn't display correctly, and vice versa for big endian. 

Little endian gives the correct depth value (499 mm) but displays the image incorrectly (with colormap jet):
![depth little endian](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22404638/43163697-dc2c90a4-8f5c-11e8-936a-d334814cb13f.png)

Big endian displays the image correctly but gives the wrong depth value (6221 mm >> 499 mm!):
![depth big endian](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22404638/43163696-dc1e369e-8f5c-11e8-9ae2-3b26d6bc52cd.png)

RealSense Viewer snapshot png:
![realsense viewer snapshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22404638/43163698-dc3a3344-8f5c-11e8-8e6a-3ab55ee5557c.png)

If I read a frame from a bag file recorded in RealSense Viewer, little endian also displays correctly (with the correct depth values), and big endian is the same (displays correctly but wrong depth values). So, this seems to be an issue with the "Save snapshot" button only.

issue_title: auto_exposure problem
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D435  | 
| Operating System & Version |   Linux (Ubuntu 16.04)  | 
| Platform                                 | PC |
| SDK Version                            |  2.0                      | 

I am using the python wrapper for a project, where I want to set the enable_auto_exposure first so that the camera can adjust to the proper exposure value, then disable the setting and confirm to use the adjusted exposure. However, it does not work. It seems like the auto_exposure only adjust something else but actually not tuning the exposure value. 

code snippets are as follows:
```
print(sensor.get_option(rs.option.exposure)
sensor.set_option(rs.option.enable_auto_exposure, 1)
# Then take some images to tune the exposure
# ...
# Then disable the setting
sensor.set_option(rs.option.enable_auto_exposure, 0)
print(sensor.get_option(rs.option.exposure)
```
 I found that the printed exposure value did not change. I also tried this in realsense-viewer, with the same idea, and save to the config.json. The value in .json still not change as above. Are there any suggestions?

issue_title: Migrating from Kinect & SDK integration w/ Microsoft?
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | n/a | 
| Firmware Version                   | n/a | 
| Operating System & Version |   Win 10 | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  n/a                                         | 
| Platform                                 | PC |
| SDK Version                            |  n/a                          | 


Hi RealSense community!

Like many others my team was using the Kinect V2 sensor (with its ecosystem of Visual Gesture Builder & Kinect Studio) to quickly train gestures.

[Since the Kinect is going extinct]([discontinued](https://www.fastcodesign.com/90147868/exclusive-microsoft-has-stopped-manufacturing-the-kinect)) and with it all of its amazing gesture training/tagging tools like [Visual Gesture Builder](https://channel9.msdn.com/Blogs/k4wdev/Custom-Gestures-End-to-End-with-Kinect-and-Visual-Gesture-Builder), we'll need to migrate to a new system.


I'm especially curious what this community has found works best for training in custom gestures.


- Are there any rough equivalents to the Kinect Visual Gesture Builder for RealSense? 
	
	ex. 

	- Record clips of actors attempting a gesture then tag/train it

	- Emit events when those gestures occur with a payload like gesture name, system confidence of the gesture, & bodyId

Also on the main [Microsoft Kinect site](https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/kinect), current users are directed to Intel's RealSense cameras & the text hints that Microsoft is working w/ Intel to "provide an option" for developers to transition away:

"*Microsoft is working with Intel to provide an option for developers looking to transition from the Kinect for Windows platform. Microsoft will continue to provide support for the Kinect for Windows SDK via our online forums, premiere and paid technical support. As developers transition from Kinect hardware, Microsoft encourages developers to look into Intel’s RealSense depth cameras.*"

- Are there any details about what this might entail?

- Specifically (and most important) what are the plans for software tools like Visual Gesture Builder to work w/ RealSense cameras under this arrangement?

issue_title: Does not show errors on later refreshes
issue_body:
After a source is changed, django-pipeline will report compilation errors the first time the product is used. However on subsequent refreshes of the page, the error simply goes away and an empty file is served up instead.

(This may be the underlying cause of the report at #605 as well.)

This is somewhat annoying when working solo, but really causes trouble working on a team. The situation usually goes like this:

* another developer/designer/tester on the team pulls down the latest code but doesn't install the latest dependencies
* compilation fails, but apparently they don't pay much attention to the initial one-time error report. Instead they start refreshing things to try get the site to work.
* things don't work since the underlying problem persists, but now all they can do is complain that the site is "all broken". They don't see any reminder of what might be wrong, or have a way to copy/paste the error for someone else to help troubleshoot.

Why does this library create an empty file when something goes wrong, instead of leaving it missing and thus recompiling again later? (Or is this a problem with one of the plugins I'm using, e.g. django-pipeline-browserify?)

issue_title: Modify the TensorFlow Scheduler and Runtime to Change the Operations Priority
issue_body:
Hi,

I am trying to modify the TensorFlow scheduler and runtime to change the operation priorities. As my understanding, TensorFlow has inter-operation and intra-operation thread pool with a scheduler scheduling operations for different threads and there is also a FIFO queue of operations for operations waiting. The workflow between them is that operations are sent to the inter-op thread pool from the executor, and then that work is running through XLA compiler to eventually be executed on the intra-op thread pool. Schedule() is in inside a specific ThreadPool and is to schedule function for the threads in the pool. The thread scheduler selects a subset of threads to run at any given moment. When the tasks are passed to the ThreadPool, they are added to one of the scheduler thread’s FIFO queues and then the scheduler will pick up the tasks distributed to the available worker threads. I have all the control and data dependencies got from the Graph in TensorFlow by using TensorBoard. Now I am not sure whether my understanding for the TensorFlow scheduler and runtime is correct or not.

The problem is that I still have no clue how and where to modify the threadpool/scheduler/ready_queue or others to change the operations priority/sequence for different threads at the TensorFlow runtime by modifying the source code. Does anyone have any ideas?

issue_title: Feature: auto-detect and prompt to renew expired temporary security credentials
issue_body:
First, I love this tool and thank you very much for making it. I'm using it in conjunction with other CLI tools like awsudo, awscli, Ansible and Terraform, and it makes it really easy to securely manage multiple AWS accounts through IAM Roles from CLI.

The only issue I have with it, is that it doesn't automatically prompt me to re-enter my Google credentials when temporary AWS security credentials issued with `AssumeRoleWithSAML` have expired.

Right now my workflow is as follows:
1. store temp creds under 'saml' profile in ~/.aws/credentials using `aws-google-auth -p saml`
1. use various CLI utilities which support shared credentials files (`~/.aws/credentials` & `~/.aws/config`)
1. get authentication error and a failed/interrupted command execution when 3600 seconds have ran out
1. repeat step 1

I would love to have this tool to also support the following workflow:
1. run `aws-google-auth -p saml -- ANY_CLI_COMMAND`
1. if temp creds stored in `~/.aws/credentials` already have or about to expire (e.g. T - 300s) prompt for Google credentials and insert new temp credentials into `~/.aws/credentials`
1. let `ANY_CLI_COMMAND` execute

For an example of such implementation see [awsudo](https://github.com/makethunder/awsudo).

issue_title: csvformat -T -U3: asks for -P but can be pleased with -Q
issue_body:
Import of tab-separated data works as expected:
```
echo 'A\tB\tC\n"1"\t2\t""3""' | in2csv -f csv -t -u3  | csvlook

| A   | B | C     |
| --- | - | ----- |
| "1" | 2 | ""3"" |
```
Export with the same settings breaks:
```shell
echo 'A\tB\tC\n"1"\t2\t""3""' | in2csv -f csv -t -u3  | csvformat -T -U3

A	B	C
need to escape, but no escapechar set
```
However, providing a dummy value for -Q makes it work again:
```
echo 'A\tB\tC\n"1"\t2\t""3""' | in2csv -f csv -t -u3  | csvformat -T -U3 -Q X
A	B	C
"1"	2	""3""
```

issue_labels: [{'id': 300688866, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20install', 'name': 'area: install', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 253794701, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20os%20x', 'name': 'area: os x', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 146954787, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'fc2929', 'default': True}, {'id': 281347824, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/has%20pr', 'name': 'has pr', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
When running `letsencrypt-auto --help --debug` `BootstrapMac` function attempts to install `pip` and `virtualenv` with this code:

```
curl --silent --show-error --retry 5 https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py |  python
pip install virtualenv
```
Since `/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/` is owned by `root` with 755 permissions installation fails with permission error.

One way to "fix" it is to chmod -R 777  site-packages packages directory as described in this blog [post](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/complete-guide-to-install-ssl-certificate-on-your-os-x-server-hosted-website/15005).

Another way is to add $SUDO to install commands:

```
curl --silent --show-error --retry 5 https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py |  $SUDO python
$SUDO pip install virtualenv
```

This appears to work (tested on Sierra 10.12.3 with both MacPorts and HomeBrew).

issue_title: Driver/ZI UHF-LI Scope trigger not armable
issue_body:
In the current version (commit b28c13d28879a8af059e0249cfcf99df32997807) of the ZI UHF-LI driver, the Scope parameter's `get` method both arms the scope trigger, awaits triggering, and transfers all the data. SInce this is blocking, it is not possible to arm the trigger, start a signal that sends a trigger using a different instrument, and only then poll the data.

I suggest splitting the current `get` method into an `arm` and `poll` method (where the latter will be called `get`). For using the scope in a loop, one would then have to `arm` at `each` loop point.

issue_title: Errors arising from lack of isolation between drivers on the same cluster.
issue_body:
Some test failures, for example https://travis-ci.org/ray-project/ray/jobs/195442413 are caused by this. In this test, two drivers both define a remote function `f`. When the second driver calls its copy of `f`, the task gets assigned to a worker and the worker executes the first driver's copy of `f`. There are a couple of problems here.

1. When two drivers both define a remote function with the same name, one of them will override the other. This happens because remote functions are currently identified by their module name plus function name. This could be fixed by incorporating the driver ID in the remote function ID (e.g., hashing them together).

2. Drivers currently have an "import counter" which counts how many things the driver has imported (e.g., remote functions, environment variables, etc). Drivers also keep track of how many things they have exported (e.g., how many remote functions they have defined). When a remote function is defined on the driver, it makes a note of how many exports were exported before it, and it will not run on a worker until that worker has imported at least that many things. However, this counter is currently shared between drivers and so exports from both drivers are incrementing a counter on the workers. This is the problem that we're running into in the link above. We need a separate counter for each driver (or we need to rethink how we're doing exports).

This is somewhat driver-centric (it won't probably handle situations where remote functions are defined on workers). Should we support that? We probably do need to support the creation of actors from tasks run on workers, so we may need to handle that a little differently.

issue_title: Coverage flapping in test_highlevel_open_tcp_listeners.py
issue_body:
There's a line in `test_highlevel_open_tcp_listeners.py` that codecov keeps complaining about, for example here (and in lots and lots of PRs): https://codecov.io/gh/python-trio/trio/pull/519/changes

The code is:

```python
            if cancel_scope.cancelled_caught:
                break
            if len(client_streams) >= limit:  # pragma: no cover
                return "lots"
    finally:
        for client_stream in client_streams:  # <---- THIS LINE
            await client_stream.aclose()

    return len(client_streams)
```

and the marked line was being reported as "partially covered". This was baffling to me, because sure, `for` loops have a branch: sometimes it executes the loop body, and sometimes it hits the end of the iterator and jumps ahead to whatever comes after the loop body. But here it was clearly doing both of those things.

So I hassled @nedbat on IRC, and he very helpfully explained: it's because of that `return "lots"` line up above. What, really? Yeah. After the `for` loop finishes, that also finishes the `finally` block, and then Python has decide where to go next. There are three possibilities:

* If the `try` block exited normally, then we move on to the next line (`return len(client_streams)`). Coverage.py saw this happen.
* If the `try` block exited with an exception, then we re-raise the exception. I guess coverage.py didn't see this happen – but coverage.py's branch coverage tracking doesn't count raising an exception as a branch, because otherwise you'd have to treat this as a possible outcome at basically every line in Python, and that would make its report useless.
* If the `try` block exited with a `return`, then after the `finally` block we immediately return, instead of executing the next line. Coverage.py didn't see this happen, and it does count it as a branch, so that was the problem.

This is quite surprising, especially since the `return` itself is marked "no cover" (which was our first attempt to solve this coverage flapping problem!), but it does make sense once you see it :-).

A major clue was clicking through codecov to find the actual XML files that coverage.py generated, where this line is described as:

```xml
<line branch="true" condition-coverage="66% (2/3)" hits="1" missing-branches="exit" number="84"/>
```

so the clues here are: (1) this `for` has *three* possible exits, instead of the normal two, and (2) the missing branch was an "exit", which I guess means an exit from the function.

Anyway, the solution is pretty easy, we should just mark more of this function as `no cover`, because it's just a silly little utility anyway. But I wanted to write this down because it was bugging me, and maybe we'll run into something like this again later.

Oh yeah and also we should fix it.

issue_title: 新增额外的文本标签
issue_body:
**V0.5.7 可用：** 基本图形新增 `extra_html_text_label` 参数用于显示额外的文本标签，仅限于在单图形或者 Page 时使用。

### 单图形

代码

```python
from pyecharts import Bar

bar = Bar("柱状图", extra_html_text_label=["bar_extra_html_text_label", "color:red"])
bar.add("商家A", CLOTHES, clothes_v1, is_stack=True)
bar.add("商家B", CLOTHES, clothes_v2, is_stack=True)
bar.render()
```

效果

![bar-demo](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19553554/43812932-31f22e0a-9af6-11e8-8fbe-c62b65daec41.png)


### Page 类

代码

```python
from pyecharts import *

page = Page()
line = Line("折线图示例", extra_html_text_label=["LINE TEXT LABEL", "color:red"])
line.add(
    "最高气温",
    WEEK,
    [11, 11, 15, 13, 12, 13, 10],
    mark_point=["max", "min"],
    mark_line=["average"],
)
page.add(line)

v1 = [11, 12, 13, 10, 10, 10]
pie = Pie("饼图-圆环图示例", title_pos="center", extra_html_text_label=["PIE TEXT LABEL"])
pie.add(
    "",
    CLOTHES,
    v1,
    radius=[40, 75],
    label_text_color=None,
    is_label_show=True,
    legend_orient="vertical",
    legend_pos="left",
)
page.add(pie)

v2 = [10, 25, 8, 60, 20, 80]
bar = Bar("柱状图", extra_html_text_label=["BAR TEXT LABEL"])
bar.add("商家B", CLOTHES, v2)
page.add(bar)
page.render()
```

效果

![page-demo](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19553554/35104305-66f2a766-fca3-11e7-8ffd-8e85911fdea5.gif)
